title: AI Adoption
content:
- [PsyArXiv Preprints | Quantifying Human-AI Synergy](https://osf.io/preprints/psyarxiv/vbkmt_v1)
- ## Adoption & Productivity
- **90%** AI adoption among software development professionals (up 14% from last year)
- **80%** of all developers surveyed report AI increased their productivity
- **59%** say AI has positively improved their code quality
- ## Trust Paradox
- **30%** of developers trust AI only a little or not at all
- 23% trust "a little"
- 7% trust "not at all"
- ## Usage Patterns
- **Median start date**: April 2024 (spike in June/July 2024 with Claude 3.5 release)
- **Median daily usage**: 2 hours
- **Tool preference**:
- 55% still using chatbots
- 41% using IDEs like Cursor
- ## Frequency of AI Use
- 39% sometimes
- 26% almost half the time
- 27% most of the time
- 7% always
- ## Task Applications
- 71% writing new code
- 66% modifying existing code
- 64% writing documentation
- 62% creating test cases
- 62% explaining concepts
- 61% analyzing data
- 59% debugging
- ## Individual Productivity Impact
- 41% slightly increased
- 31% moderately increased
- 13% extremely increased
- 9% no impact
- 3% slightly decreased
- ## Code Quality Impact
- 31% slightly improved
- 21% moderately improved
- 7% extremely improved
- 30% no impact
- 7% slightly worsened
- ## Organizational Findings
- Individual effectiveness
- Organizational performance
- Valuable work
- Team performance
- ## Team Archetypes
- **Legacy Bottleneck** (11% of respondents): Teams in reactive state with unstable systems, low product performance, high friction and burnout
- ## Critical Organizational Challenge
- Quality of internal platform
- Clarity of workflows
- Alignment of teams
- Strategic focus on underlying organizational systems
- ## DORA AI Capabilities Model
- Clear and communicated AI stance
- Healthy data ecosystems
- AI-accessible internal data
- Strong version control practices
- Working in small batches
- User-centric focus
- Quality internal platforms
- ## Methodology
- Nearly 5,000 technology professionals surveyed globally
- Hundreds of hours of qualitative data
- Survey conducted July 2024
